steps.title: Use vertex form of quadratic
steps.content: The quadratic models a projectile's height, reaching maximum at vertex. Vertex form: $$ h(t) = a(t - 5)^2 + 400 $$. The vertex (5, 400) represents the max height at 5 seconds.

steps.title: Find coefficient 'a' using initial condition
steps.content: At $ t = 0 $, height is 0: $$ 0 = a(0 - 5)^2 + 400 $$. Solving: $$ 0 = 25a + 400 \Rightarrow a = -16 $$. Equation becomes: $ h(t) = -16(t - 5)^2 + 400 $.

steps.title: Calculate height at t = 9 seconds
steps.content: Substitute $ t = 9 $: $$ h(9) = -16(4)^2 + 400 = -256 + 400 = 144 $$. The answer is 144.
